text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in building reliable applications. Unit tests check individual functions or components in isolation, ensuring each part works correctly. Integration tests then verify that these components work together smoothly, catching issues in how different modules interact. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps clarify requirements and prevents bugs early. Together, these testing practices give developers confidence to make changes or add features without fear of breaking existing functionality. A well-tested system is easier to maintain and more dependable for users.
